about.title: About Us
about.metaDescription: Who's behind WhatsUsernames.link — a free, independent, stateless tool for turning WhatsApp usernames and phone numbers into shareable links and QR codes.
about.intro: WhatsUsernames.link is a small, independently-run project built to solve one specific problem: WhatsApp is rolling out @usernames, but there was no easy way to turn one into a shareable link people can tap to start chatting.

sections.heading: What we built
body: WhatsUsernames.link turns your WhatsApp Username or phone number into a wa.me link, a shorter whatsusernames.link link, and a downloadable QR code — entirely in your browser. Beyond the generator, the site also offers a free public REST API, a TypeScript SDK, and tools for the WhatsApp Business Platform's Business-Scoped User IDs (BSUID), all built the same way: free, keyless, and stateless.

sections.heading: Why we built it
body: As WhatsApp's username rollout reached more regions in 2026, there was still no official way to share a clickable link built from a username, and no clear documentation for developers who wanted to build on top of it. This site fills that gap with a straightforward tool and open documentation, and stays current as WhatsApp's own rollout evolves.

sections.heading: Who's behind it
body: The project is built and maintained by Ruben, an independent developer. It isn't a company or a team, just one person keeping the tool working, writing the guides, and answering questions.

sections.heading: How it's run
body: There's no account system and no database: everything you enter runs in your browser and is never sent to or stored on our servers. See our Privacy Policy for details. The site is free to use and funded by ads (Adsterra and Google AdSense) rather than subscriptions.

sections.heading: Independent project
body: WhatsUsernames.link is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or associated with WhatsApp Inc. or Meta Platforms, Inc. All product names and trademarks belong to their respective owners.

sections.heading: Open source
body: The code that powers this site, including the API and SDK, is open source on GitHub at github.com/insidedcpulse-spec/whatsuser-link. Contributions, bug reports, and suggestions are welcome.

sections.heading: Get in touch
body: Questions, feedback, or a bug to report? Visit our Contact page.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.metaDescription: Get in touch with WhatsUsernames.link for questions, feedback, or bug reports.
contact.intro: Have a question, found a bug, or want to suggest a feature? Here's how to reach us.

sections.heading: Email
body: The fastest way to reach us is by email: insidedcpulse@gmail.com. We read every message and aim to reply within a few days.

sections.heading: GitHub
body: For bug reports or feature requests related to the code, API, or SDK, open an issue on our GitHub repository at github.com/insidedcpulse-spec/whatsuser-link, where the project's source is public.

sections.heading: Response times
body: This is an independently-run project maintained by one person, so replies may take a few days, especially for detailed technical questions.

sections.heading: Before you write
body: Looking for API documentation? Check the Developers page. Questions about data and privacy are answered in our Privacy Policy.

developers.title: Free WhatsApp Link API for Developers
developers.metaDescription: Free public REST API to generate WhatsApp links and QR codes from usernames or phone numbers. No API key, no signup. JSON responses, CORS enabled.
developers.intro: Generate WhatsApp links, validate usernames and phone numbers, and render QR codes over plain HTTP GET. No API key, no signup, free. All endpoints return JSON (the QR endpoint returns an image), send CORS headers, and are safe to call from browsers.
developers.endpointsIntro: Base URL: {baseUrl}. All endpoints are GET.

endpointDescriptions.usernameLink: WhatsApp link + short link from a username (optional key and prefilled text).
endpointDescriptions.phoneLink: Official wa.me click-to-chat link from an international phone number.
endpointDescriptions.validateUsername: Check whether a username is structurally valid.
endpointDescriptions.validateKey: Check whether a Username Key is valid (4-8 letters/numbers).
endpointDescriptions.validatePhone: Check whether a phone number is valid for WhatsApp links.
endpointDescriptions.qr: QR code (PNG or SVG) for a username or phone link. Custom size and colors.
endpointDescriptions.openapi: Machine-readable OpenAPI 3.1 description of this API.
endpointDescriptions.businessBsuidValidate: Check whether a Business-Scoped User ID (BSUID) is structurally valid.
endpointDescriptions.businessBsuidParse: Parse a BSUID into its country code, id, and parent-account flag.
endpointDescriptions.businessUsernameValidate: Check whether a WhatsApp Business Platform username is structurally valid.
endpointDescriptions.businessContactResolve: Resolve a contact from exactly one of bsuid, phone, or username into a single unified shape.
endpointDescriptions.businessWebhookNormalize: Normalize a raw WhatsApp Cloud API webhook payload (messages or status updates) into a consistent event shape.

developers.businessEndpointsIntro: Base URL: {baseUrl}. These endpoints are POST with a JSON body, for the WhatsApp Business Platform / Cloud API (BSUID, business usernames, contact resolution, webhook normalization). Same CORS, rate limits, and error format as the endpoints above.
developers.rateLimitBody: 60 requests per minute per IP for JSON endpoints, 20 per minute for the QR endpoint. Responses include X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset headers; exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header. Identical requests are served from CDN cache and don't count against the limit.
developers.errorsBody: Errors are always JSON with a stable machine-readable code: for example a bad username returns HTTP 400 with a body like the one below. Codes never change within v1.
developers.noticeBody: WhatsApp's username feature is in a phased regional rollout, and wa.me/<username> links may not open a chat for every account yet. The username-link endpoint includes a notice field with the current status. Phone-number links (wa.me/<phone>) are officially documented and work everywhere today.

metadata.title: WhatsApp Username Link Generator + QR Code (Free) | WhatsUsernames.link
metadata.description: Create a shareable link and QR code for your WhatsApp Username in seconds. Simple, fast, and free.

items.question: How do I create a WhatsApp link from my username?
items.answer: Open WhatsApp, go to Settings → Profile, tap your name, and set a Username (WhatsApp lets you pick one and change it occasionally). Come back here, enter that exact same username in the generator above, and WhatsUsernames.link instantly builds three things: a wa.me/<username> link in WhatsApp's own documented format, a shorter whatsusernames.link/<username> link that's easier to share verbally or on a business card, and a downloadable QR code in PNG, JPEG, SVG, or PDF. None of this needs an account, an email address, or a WhatsApp Business API integration — it all runs in your browser and nothing you type is saved on our servers. Because WhatsApp's username feature is still rolling out region by region, some people who click your link today may need to search for your username manually inside the app until the rollout reaches their area.

items.question: What is WhatsUsernames.link?
items.answer: WhatsUsernames.link is a free, independent tool that turns your WhatsApp Username or phone number into a shareable wa.me link and a matching QR code, so people can start a conversation with you without ever needing to save your number in their contacts. It's built to be stateless: everything runs in your browser, nothing you enter is stored on our servers, and there's no account or signup required. Beyond the link/QR generator, the site also publishes a free, keyless public REST API and a TypeScript SDK for developers who want the same link/QR/validation logic in their own apps, plus tools for the WhatsApp Business Platform's Business-Scoped User IDs (BSUID). It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or associated with WhatsApp Inc. or Meta.

items.question: Is it free?
items.answer: Yes. Generating your link and QR code is completely free and doesn't require signing up.

items.question: Does wa.me/username work yet?
items.answer: Not for most people yet. As of 2026-07-04, WhatsApp's Help Center documents wa.me/<username> (no /u/ prefix) as the official link format, so the format itself is real and confirmed. What's still missing is the server-side resolution behind it: WhatsApp is rolling it out region by region — a first wave of countries from 2026-07-07, a second wave from 2026-07-20, and the rest from September 2026 — so clicking a username link today may do nothing or show an error outside those regions. Until your region is covered, whoever receives your link needs to open WhatsApp and search for your username manually. That's exactly why we also offer a phone-number generator: wa.me/<phone> is officially documented and works everywhere today, with no rollout to wait for.

items.question: What is the Username Key?
items.answer: It's an optional code WhatsApp lets you attach to your username, useful when several people may have similar usernames. If you've set one, you can share it together with your link.

items.question: Is WhatsUsernames.link affiliated with WhatsApp or Meta?
items.answer: No. We're an independent tool, not affiliated with, endorsed by, or associated with WhatsApp Inc. or Meta.

items.question: What is wa.me?
items.answer: wa.me is WhatsApp's official short domain for links that open a chat, such as wa.me/1234567890 for a phone number. WhatsUsernames.link builds these links for you automatically.

items.question: How do I create a WhatsApp link without saving the contact first?
items.answer: Generate a link with your phone number or username here, then share it. Anyone who opens the link can message you on WhatsApp without adding your number to their contacts first.

items.question: Is my phone number or username stored anywhere?
items.answer: No. WhatsUsernames.link is deliberately stateless: there is no database, no user accounts, and no server-side storage of anything you type. Your phone number, username, Username Key, and any pre-filled message stay in your browser the entire time — the link and QR code are built client-side using WhatsApp's own documented wa.me format, not sent to us for processing or saved anywhere. Closing the tab or refreshing the page erases everything instantly, since there was never a copy kept elsewhere. This also means we can't recover a link for you later if you lose it — you'd simply regenerate it by entering the same username or number again. See our Privacy Policy for the full breakdown of what little data (like anonymous analytics) our hosting and ad provider process.

items.question: Can I use this for a WhatsApp Business account?
items.answer: Yes. The generator works the same way for personal and WhatsApp Business numbers and usernames.

items.question: How do I create a WhatsApp QR code?
items.answer: Enter your username or phone number in the generator above — either works, so pick whichever you'd rather share. As soon as you generate the link, a matching QR code appears automatically in the panel below it; there's no separate tool or extra step. From there you can customize the QR code's color to match your branding, choose a transparent or solid background, and download it in whichever format suits your use case: PNG for social media and screens, JPEG as a lighter alternative, SVG if you're printing at large scale like a poster or storefront sign without losing sharpness, or PDF if you're sending it straight to a print shop. The QR code always encodes the same wa.me link shown above it, so scanning it opens exactly the same WhatsApp conversation.

guide.title: How to Create a WhatsApp Link From Your Username
guide.metaDescription: Step-by-step guide to creating a shareable WhatsApp link from your Username, including what to do until wa.me/username officially works.
guide.intro: WhatsApp is rolling out @Username handles, letting people message you without knowing your phone number. Here's how to turn yours into a link and QR code you can share anywhere.

steps.title: Set your WhatsApp Username
steps.description: In the WhatsApp app, go to Settings → Profile and set your Username (requires an app version that supports the 2026 rollout).

steps.title: Add a Username Key (optional)
steps.description: If WhatsApp assigns you a Username Key to disambiguate you from others with the same username, note it down.

steps.title: Generate your link
steps.description: Paste your Username (and Key, if you have one) into the generator on this site.

steps.title: Copy your link or download your QR code
steps.description: Use "Copy all" to share your username and key together, or download the QR code as PNG, JPEG, SVG, or PDF.

steps.title: Share it anywhere
steps.description: Add it to your bio, business card, email signature, or storefront.

guide.noteHeading: Important: wa.me/username's regional rollout means it may not open a chat yet
guide.note: wa.me/<username> is the link format WhatsApp has documented, but it's still in a phased regional rollout, so it doesn't open a chat for most users yet. Whoever receives your link or QR code may need to open WhatsApp and search for your username manually until the rollout reaches their region. If you need a link that works today, use the phone-number generator on the home page instead.
guide.ctaText: Generate your WhatsApp Username link now.
guide.linkLabel: How to create a WhatsApp link

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.metaDescription: How WhatsUsernames.link handles data: what we don't collect, what our hosting and ad provider process, and your rights.
privacy.intro: WhatsUsernames.link ("we", "us") operates whatsusernames.link. This page explains what little data is involved when you use this free tool, and why.

sections.heading: This tool is stateless
body: WhatsUsernames.link builds your WhatsApp link and QR code entirely in your browser. The WhatsApp Username, Username Key, phone number, and message you enter are never sent to or stored on our servers. Closing or refreshing the page erases them.

sections.heading: No accounts, no sign-up
body: There is no registration, login, or user account. We have no database of users or usernames.

sections.heading: Server logs
body: Like any website, our hosting provider (Vercel) processes standard technical logs to serve pages — IP address, browser type, and request timestamps. These are used only for security, abuse prevention, and reliability, and we don't link them to any personal profile.

sections.heading: Cookies and local storage
body: We don't set tracking cookies ourselves. Your light/dark theme preference is saved only in your browser's local storage and never leaves your device.

sections.heading: Advertising
body: This site shows ads served by Adsterra and Google AdSense, third-party advertising networks. Google, as a third-party vendor, uses cookies to serve ads on this site; Google's use of advertising cookies enables it and its partners to serve ads based on your visits to this site and/or other sites on the Internet. You may opt out of personalized advertising by visiting Google's Ads Settings (adssettings.google.com). Adsterra's ad content may use similar cookies or technologies to serve and measure ads. We don't control either network's data. If you prefer not to see ads or be measured by them, review each network's own privacy practices, use Google's opt-out, or use an ad blocker / your browser's tracking protection.

sections.heading: Analytics
body: We don't currently run any first-party analytics or tracking script. If this changes, this policy will be updated first.

sections.heading: Your rights
body: Because we don't collect or store personal data on our servers, there is generally nothing for us to access, correct, or delete. If you believe we hold data about you, contact us using the details below and we'll look into it.

sections.heading: Children's privacy
body: This tool isn't directed at children, and we don't knowingly collect information from children.

sections.heading: Changes to this policy
body: We may update this page as the tool evolves. The date above reflects the latest revision.

sections.heading: Contact
body: Questions about this policy? Email insidedcpulse@gmail.com.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.metaDescription: Terms for using WhatsUsernames.link's free WhatsApp link and QR code generator.
terms.intro: By using whatsusernames.link ("the Service"), you agree to these terms. If you don't agree, please don't use the Service.

sections.heading: What this Service does
body: WhatsUsernames.link is a free tool that generates a shareable WhatsApp link and QR code from a WhatsApp Username, Username Key, or phone number you provide, entirely in your browser.

sections.heading: No account, free to use
body: The Service is 100% free, with no signup and no payment required for its current features.

sections.heading: Accuracy is your responsibility
body: You're responsible for entering your own correct username, phone number, and message. We don't verify identity or ownership of any username or phone number entered into the generator.

sections.heading: Acceptable use
body: Don't use the Service to generate links for numbers or usernames you don't have the right to share, or for spam, harassment, fraud, or impersonation.

sections.heading: wa.me/username limitation
body: As of the date of these terms, wa.me/username links may not reliably open a WhatsApp chat for everyone, because WhatsApp's username rollout is regional and phased. We make no guarantee that this or any generated link will behave as expected, and we provide a phone-number generator as a currently working alternative.

sections.heading: No warranty
body: The Service is provided "as is," without warranty of any kind, including uptime, accuracy, or fitness for a particular purpose.

sections.heading: Limitation of liability
body: To the extent permitted by law, we aren't liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from use of the Service.

sections.heading: Third-party links and ads
body: The Service links to and displays content from third parties we don't control and aren't responsible for, including advertising served by Adsterra and WhatsApp itself.

sections.heading: No affiliation
body: WhatsUsernames.link is an independent tool, not affiliated with, endorsed by, or associated with WhatsApp Inc. or Meta Platforms, Inc.

sections.heading: Changes
body: We may update these terms as the Service evolves. Continued use after changes means you accept the current version.

sections.heading: Governing law
body: These terms are governed by the laws of Portugal, without prejudice to any mandatory consumer-protection law of your country of residence.

sections.heading: Contact
body: Questions about these terms? Email insidedcpulse@gmail.com.
